title: Net Interest Margin Squeeze Playbook
content: When Net Interest Margin (NIM) compresses by more than 15 bps MoM: 1. Audit funding costs of deposit accounts. 2. Verify interest rate sensitivities (duration gap analysis). 3. Simulate What-if lending rate adjustments on new loans to offset yield compression. 4. Pivot asset allocations toward higher-yielding commercial credit portfolios.

title: NPL Risk Mitigation Standard
content: When the Non-Performing Loan (NPL) ratio exceeds 1.5% in any retail branch segment: 1. Deploy the Credit Score Classifier model on active accounts. 2. Perform a Root Cause Analysis (RCA) across customer demographic slices. 3. Adjust underwriting criteria for loan applicants scoring below 650. 4. Accelerate early-stage collections workflow actions.

title: LDR Liquidity Audit Protocol
content: To prevent liquidity squeezes, the standard Loan-to-Deposit Ratio (LDR) audit involves: 1. Gathering active loan portfolios and core deposit volumes. 2. Verifying if the ratio crosses the 85% regulatory trigger. 3. Running multi-channel alert flows if deposits drop and loans grow. 4. Scheduling automatic asset-liability committee (ALCO) reviews.
